creator: areilly@bigpond.net.au
text:
Hi Greg,

Thanks for looking at this.

On Mon, May 12, 2008 at 08:40:10PM -0700, Greg Lewis wrote:
> On Mon, May 12, 2008 at 11:53:39AM -0000, Andrew Reilly wrote:
> > /usr/bin/g++    -fPIC -DCC_NOEX -W -Wall  -Wno-unused -Wno-parentheses =
  -I../../../../src/plugin/solaris/nscore -I../../../../src/plugin/solaris/=
nscore/../common -I/usr/ports/java/jdk16/work/control/build/bsd-amd64/tmp/d=
eploy/plugin/ -I/usr/ports/java/jdk16/work/control/build/bsd-amd64/tmp/depl=
oy/plugin/CClassHeaders -I../../../../src/plugin/solaris/common -I../../../=
../src/plugin/share/jpiapi -I../../../../src/plugin/solaris/nscore/../nsstu=
b -I/usr/local/include -I/usr/local/include -I../../../../../j2se/src/solar=
is/javavm/export -I../../../../../j2se/src/share/javavm/export -I../../../.=
./../j2se/src/share/javavm/include -I../../../../../j2se/src/solaris/javavm=
/include  -D_GNU_SOURCE -DXP_UNIX -DNO_XEMBED_MOZILLA -DPLUGIN_NODOTVERSION=
=3D\"160_03\" -DPLUGIN_VERSION=3D\"1.6.0_03\" -DVERSION=3D\"1.6.0_03-p4-b00=
\" -DRELEASE=3D\"1.6.0_03-p4\" -DARCH=3D\"amd64\" -DLIBARCH=3D\"amd64\" -D_=
ALLBSD_SOURCE -DRAPTOR_API -DNEW_STREAMING_API -DXP_UNIX -D_LP64=3D1  -c -o=
 /usr/ports/java/jdk16/work/control/build/bsd-amd64/tmp/deploy/plugin/javap=
lugin_nscp/obj64/remotejni.o  ../../../../src/plugin/solaris/nscore/remotej=
ni.cpp
> > ../../../../src/plugin/solaris/nscore/remotejni.cpp: In function 'void =
jni_CallVoidMethod(RemoteJNIEnv*, _jobject*, _jmethodID*, ...)':
> > ../../../../src/plugin/solaris/nscore/remotejni.cpp:881: error: 'JNI_EI=
NVAL' was not declared in this scope
>=20
> Somewhere in the include path (my bet is /usr/local/include) you have a
> bogus jni.h.  A quick fix would be to get rid of whatever package is
> installing that.  A better fix would be to modify the include path for
> the plugin build to put that last.

locate jni.h shows that there are several on my system:
/usr/local/include/firefox/java/jni.h
/usr/local/include/firefox/jni.h
/usr/local/include/jni.h
/usr/local/include/mozilla/java/jni.h
/usr/local/include/mozilla/jni.h
/usr/local/jdk1.5.0/include/jni.h
/usr/local/jdk1.6.0/debug/include/jni.h
/usr/local/jdk1.6.0/include/jni.h
/usr/local/lib/sablevm/include/jni.h
/usr/ports/graphics/gnash/work/gnash-0.8.1/plugin/mozilla-sdk/include/jni.h

The one in /usr/local/include belongs to sablevm-1.13_2,
according to pkg_info -W, so I'll remove that and see how things
go from there...

/usr/local/lib/sablevm/include/jni.h is a symlink to the
/usr/local/include/jni.h file.  I guess that the sablevm
maintainer should think about just keeping the port-specific
version...?

[I can submit a pr to that effect, if you think it appropriate.]

Cheers,

--=20
Andrew
